Module 9 - Lesson 3
Understanding 	Your Tracking Tool Metrics	:

-- 1 of 8 --

Module 9 - Lesson 3
Understanding 	Your Tracking Tool Metrics	:
Clicks - Unique Visitors: 	The unique number of people that have
visited a webpage.!
!
CPC - Cost per click: 	The price of each acquired visitor. This statistic
allows you to determine how much 	it cost you to generate a visitor.

-- 2 of 8 --

Module 9 - Lesson 3
Understanding 	Your Tracking Tool Metrics	:
Actions - Unique Actions: 	The total number of actions generated as a
result of the visitors.!
!
C2A - Percentage of Clicks to Actions: 	Determines the percent of
visitors that were then converted into opt-ins. For instance, if the system
showed 72% for C2A, it would mean that on average, out of every 100
people that visit your website, 72 of them opt-in.

-- 3 of 8 --

Module 9 - Lesson 3
Understanding 	Your Tracking Tool Metrics	:
CPA - Cost per action: 	Determines how much it cost you to generate
an opt-in.

-- 4 of 8 --

Module 9 - Lesson 3
Understanding 	Your Tracking Tool Metrics	:
Sales - Number of sales: 	The total number of sales generated from a
particular campaign.!
!
Sales Value - Total sales value: 	The total sales value is calculated as
(total sales x sales value) and will show the gross income produced as a
direct result from a particular campaign. (When you setup a new
campaign, you are asked to enter the value of your product.)

-- 5 of 8 --

Module 9 - Lesson 3
Understanding 	Your Tracking Tool Metrics	:
CPS - Cost per sale: 	Calculates how much it cost you to generate a
each sale (calculated as Campaign Cost/Total # of Sales). 	!
!
C2S - Percentage of Clicks to Sales: 	Represents the percent of unique
visitors that were converted into sales. Calculated as (number of sales
divided by the number of unique visitors) multiplied by 100.

-- 6 of 8 --

Module 9 - Lesson 3
Understanding 	Your Tracking Tool Metrics	:
Cost - Cost of campaign: 	The total amount of money invested into this
campaign.!
!
Profit - Gross profit from campaign: 	The total amount of gross profit
calculated as 	(total sales - campaign cost).

-- 7 of 8 --

Module 9 - Lesson 3
Understanding 	Your Tracking Tool Metrics	:
ROI - Return on Investment: 	Calculates the percentage of your
campaign cost returned as sales. ROI is calculated as (sales value /
campaign cost) * 100. 	!
!
A 100% return on investment would mean that 100% of your campaign
cost was returned with no profit being generated. Anything above 100%
represents a profit. Likewise, anything below 100% represents a loss
based on the campaign cost.
The most
important number!

-- 8 of 8 --
